A tree is balanced if for every node, the height difference between its left and right subtrees is at most 1.
You can compute the height of a subtree recursively. The height of a null node is 0.
A naive approach checks balance at each node by computing heights top-down, but this leads to redundant computation.
For an optimal O(N) solution, combine height computation and balance checking into a single recursive function. Return -1 as a sentinel to indicate an unbalanced subtree.
